message:
refactor(raptorq): simplify GF256 scalar XOR to unified pointer walk

Replace the 32-byte/16-byte/1-byte chunked iteration in
gf256_add_slices2_scalar() with a single unified pointer walk that
processes both (dst_a ^ src_a) and (dst_b ^ src_b) in one pass using
8-byte u64 words followed by a byte-level tail. This eliminates the
layered chunks_exact_mut()/chunks_exact() iterator nesting and reduces
the function from ~40 lines to ~15 lines while maintaining identical
semantics. Also adds #[inline] and removes the now-unnecessary
clippy::similar_names allow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
